message.content:
{
  "competency_groups": [
    {
      "code": "C1",
      "title": "Physical World and Measurement",
      "description": "Competencies related to units of measurement, SI systems, significant figures, dimensional analysis, and uncertainty determination.",
      "competencies": [
        "Understand the need for measurement and distinguish between fundamental and derived units within SI system.",
        "Apply rules for significant figures and determine uncertainty in results.",
        "Perform dimensional analysis to verify physical equations and derive relationships."
      ],
      "unit_name": "Unit I: Physical World and Measurements",
      "chapter_name": "Units and Measurements"
    },
    {
      "code": "C2",
      "title": "Kinematics - Straight Line Motion",
      "description": "Competencies involving frame of reference, differentiation/integration for motion description, uniform/non-uniform motion, and graphical analysis.",
      "competencies": [
        "Describe motion using elementary concepts of differentiation and integration.",
        "Distinguish between average speed/velocity and instantaneous velocity in non-uniform motion.",
        "Analyze uniformly accelerated motion using relations derived graphically or via calculus."
      ],
      "unit_name": "Unit II: Kinematics",
      "chapter_name": "Motion in a Straight Line"
    },
    {
      "code": "C3",
      "title": "Kinematics - Motion in a Plane",
      "description": "Competencies covering vector operations, resolution of vectors, and motion under uniform velocity/acceleration including projectile and circular motion.",
      "competencies": [
        "Perform addition, subtraction, scalar multiplication, and dot/cross products on vectors.",
        "Resolve vectors into rectangular components in a plane.",
        "Analyze projectile motion and uniform circular motion using vector principles."
      ],
      "unit_name": "Unit II: Kinematics",
      "chapter_name": "Motion in a Plane"
    },
    {
      "code": "C4",
      "title": "Laws of Motion",
      "description": "Competencies regarding Newton's laws, momentum conservation, friction types, and dynamics of circular motion.",
      "competencies": [
        "Apply Newton's three laws to explain force, inertia, impulse, and momentum changes.",
        "Utilize the law of conservation of linear momentum in collision scenarios.",
        "Analyze equilibrium conditions for concurrent forces and distinguish between static, kinetic, and rolling friction."
      ],
      "unit_name": "Unit III: Laws of Motion",
      "chapter_name": "Laws of Motion"
    },
    {
      "code": "C5",
      "title": "Work, Energy and Power",
      "description": "Competencies involving work done by constant/variable forces, energy theorems, potential energy concepts, and collision types.",
      "competencies": [
        "Calculate work done by constant and variable forces and relate it to kinetic energy via the work-energy theorem.",
        "Differentiate between conservative and non-conservative forces and analyze motion in vertical circles.",
        "Distinguish between elastic and inelastic collisions in one and two dimensions."
      ],
      "unit_name": "Unit IV: Work, Energy and Power",
      "chapter_name": "Work, Energy and Power"
    },
    {
      "code": "C6",
      "title": "System of Particles and Rotational Motion",
      "description": "Competencies covering center of mass concepts, torque, angular momentum conservation, moment of inertia, and rotational dynamics.",
      "competencies": [
        "Determine the center of mass for particle systems and rigid bodies like uniform rods.",
        "Apply laws of conservation of linear and angular momentum to physical scenarios.",
        "Compare linear and rotational motions using torque, moment of inertia, and equations of rotational motion."
      ],
      "unit_name": "Unit V: Motion of System of Particles and Rigid Body",
      "chapter_name": "System of Particles and Rotational Motion"
    },
    {
      "code": "C7",
      "title": "Gravitation",
      "description": "Competencies involving Kepler's laws, universal gravitation, gravitational potential/energy, orbital mechanics, and variation in gravity.",
      "competencies": [
        "Apply Kepler's laws of planetary motion to describe celestial movements.",
        "Calculate acceleration due to gravity variations with altitude and depth using the universal law of gravitation.",
        "Determine escape speed and orbital velocity for satellites based on energy considerations."
      ],
      "unit_name": "Unit VI: Gravitation",
      "chapter_name": "Gravitation"
    },
    {
      "code": "C8",
      "title": "Mechanical Properties of Solids",
      "description": "Competencies regarding elasticity, stress-strain relationships, material moduli, and elastic energy.",
      "competencies": [
        "Interpret Hooke's law and define Young’s modulus, bulk modulus, shear modulus, and Poisson's ratio.",
        "Analyze the relationship between stress and strain in solids under various loads."
      ],
      "unit_name": "Unit VII: Properties of Bulk Matter",
      "chapter_name": "Mechanical Properties of Solids"
    },
    {
      "code": "C9",
      "title": "Mechanical Properties of Fluids",
      "description": "Competencies covering fluid pressure, Pascal's law, viscosity, Bernoulli's theorem, and surface tension phenomena.",
      "competencies": [
        "Apply Pascal's law to hydraulic systems and analyze fluid pressure due to gravity.",
        "Explain viscous flow concepts including Stokes' law, terminal velocity, and Reynolds number (critical velocity).",
        "Use Bernoulli's theorem for applications like Torricelli's law and dynamic lift."
      ],
      "unit_name": "Unit VII: Properties of Bulk Matter",
      "chapter_name": "Mechanical Properties of Fluids"
    },
    {
      "code": "C10",
      "title": "Thermal Properties of Matter",
      "description": "Competencies involving heat transfer modes, thermal expansion, specific heats, latent heat, and blackbody radiation laws.",
      "competencies": [
        "Distinguish between conduction, convection, and radiation in heat transfer processes.",
        "Analyze anomalous expansion of water and calculate calorimetric quantities (Cp, Cv).",
        "Apply Wien's displacement law and Stefan's law to blackbody radiation problems."
      ],
      "unit_name": "Unit VII: Properties of Bulk Matter",
      "chapter_name": "Thermal Properties of Matter"
    },
    {
      "code": "C11",
      "title": "Thermodynamics",
      "description": "Competencies covering laws of thermodynamics, state variables, and processes like isothermal/adiabatic changes.",
      "competencies": [
        "Apply the Zeroth law to define thermal equilibrium and temperature measurement.",
        "Utilize the First Law (conservation of energy) for heat, work, and internal energy calculations.",
        "Analyze reversible, irreversible, isothermal, adiabatic, and cyclic processes using thermodynamic state variables."
      ],
      "unit_name": "Unit VIII: Thermodynamics",
      "chapter_name": "Thermodynamics"
    },
    {
      "code": "C12",
      "title": "Kinetic Theory of Gases",
      "description": "Competencies involving the equation of state, kinetic assumptions, molecular speed concepts, and degrees of freedom.",
      "competencies": [
        "Derive pressure from kinetic theory assumptions and relate it to temperature via rms speed.",
        "Apply the law of equipartition of energy to specific heat capacities of gases."
      ],
      "unit_name": "Unit IX: Behaviour of Perfect Gases and Kinetic Theory of Gases",
      "chapter_name": "Kinetic Theory"
    },
    {
      "code": "C13",
      "title": "Oscillations",
      "description": "Competencies regarding periodic motion, simple harmonic motion (SHM), energy in oscillation, and pendulum dynamics.",
      "competencies": [
        "Identify conditions for Simple Harmonic Motion and derive equations of motion from uniform circular motion concepts.",
        "Analyze the restoring force and time period of a loaded spring and a simple pendulum."
      ],
      "unit_name": "Unit X: Oscillations and Waves",
      "chapter_name": "Oscillations"
    },
    {
      "code": "C14",
      "title": "Waves",
      "description": "Competencies covering wave types, superposition principle, standing waves, resonance modes, and beats.",
      "competencies": [
        "Distinguish between transverse and longitudinal waves and describe displacement relations for progressive waves."
      ],
      "unit_name": "Unit X: Oscillations and Waves",
      "chapter_name": "Waves"
    }
  ]
}
